meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master Debugging: Design with AI for Intermediate Students
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug your code? Learn how to leverage AI-driven design patterns with Antigravity and Claude Code to build robust, professional-grade software.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at cryptic stack traces. By shifting your workflow from manual debugging to AI-assisted design, you treat code as a modular system rather than a monolith. Use Antigravity to architect your logic flow and Claude Code to iterate on implementation, effectively neutralizing bugs before they are ever written.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the cursor blinks, and your console is bleeding red error messages. You’ve been here for hours—trapped in a labyrinth of syntax errors and logic flaws that seem to multiply the moment you patch them. It’s the rite of passage for every student, but it doesn't have to be your ceiling.

True engineering isn't about memorizing every edge case; it's about mastering the leverage you have at your disposal. By integrating AI design patterns into your workflow, you move from being a frustrated debugger to a high-level architect. You aren't just fixing bugs; you're designing them out of existence.

steps.title: Architecting the Logic Flow
steps.description: Before writing code, use Antigravity to map your system architecture. This prevents logic-based bugs from taking root in your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project requirements and generate a modular architecture diagram. Identify potential race conditions and suggest a data flow that minimizes state complexity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/architecture.md --ask 'Validate this architecture for potential memory leaks.'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Feed your existing file into Claude Code to identify anti-patterns that trigger common debugging nightmares.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this snippet for 'code smells' and suggest a refactor that adheres to SOLID principles.

steps.title: Test-Driven AI Generation
steps.description: Generate comprehensive unit tests first to define expected outcomes, making debugging a matter of following the test results.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of Jest tests for this function, covering edge cases including null inputs and asynchronous failures.

steps.title: Isolating State Bugs
steps.description: Use AI to trace state changes in complex components, identifying exactly where your data flow breaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trace the state variable 'userSession' throughout this file and identify where it becomes undefined.

steps.title: Refactoring for Maintainability
steps.description: Apply AI-driven refactoring to simplify complex logic, reducing the surface area for future bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this complex conditional block into a strategy pattern to improve readability and debuggability.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Success Criteria' in a comment block at the top of your file; it anchors the AI's context.
Treat your error logs as data: Feed the raw stack trace directly into Claude Code rather than summarizing it yourself.
Use Antigravity to generate 'Negative Test Cases'—code that is intentionally wrong—to verify your error handling triggers correctly.
When refactoring, perform it in small, atomic commits; it makes reverting a bad AI suggestion instantaneous.
Leverage Claude Code's ability to read multiple files simultaneously to debug cross-module dependency issues.
content.next_steps: Now that you have automated your debugging workflow, begin documenting your process. Create a 'Build Log' for your next project to track how AI design patterns reduced your development time.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the methodology to stop fighting your code and start commanding it. Transition from student to builder by applying these patterns to your current project today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
